meaning: n.[植]没药树,没药(没药树的树胶脂)
examples: Myrrh was a spice used to embalm the dead.
I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
Gold, frankincense and myrrh were the three gifts the Magi gave to Jesus.
The fragrances are technically unisex, but scents such as the sweet myrrh-laced Homero skew male, while the magnolia-based Malena and jasmine-accented Amalia are more feminine.
